revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:本朝皇胤紹運録]]
'''Honchō Kōin Shōunroku''' (本朝皇胤紹運録) is a genealogical record of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=emperors|1=Emperor of Japan}} and [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]]. Also referred to as '''Shōunroku''' (紹運録), '''Shōunzu''' (紹運図), '''Honchō Teikō Shōunroku''' (本朝帝皇紹運録), '''Teiō Gokeizu''' (帝王御系図), or '''Teikō Keifu''' (帝皇系譜), it is considered one of the most authoritative genealogical texts of the [[:en:Japanese imperial family|Japanese imperial family]].<ref>小倉慈司『本朝皇胤紹運録』写本の基礎的研究,2011</ref> Before the establishment of the {{ill|Kōtōfu|ja|皇統譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtōfu|1=Record of Imperial Lineage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581096}}, the numbering of emperors was generally based on this text.<ref name="katayama">片山杜秀『尊王攘夷―水戸学四百年の歴史』2021、p.197。</ref>
The text was commissioned by {{ill|Emperor Go-Komatsu|en|Emperor Go-Komatsu|qid=Q123052|lt=Emperor Go-Komatsu|1=Emperor Go-Komatsu}} and compiled by the then-{{ill|Naidaijin|en|Naidaijin|qid=Q1185338|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Naidaijin}} {{ill|Tōin Mitsumune|ja|洞院満季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōin Mitsumune|1=Tōin Mitsusue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17219398}}. Mitsumune compared many imperial genealogies circulating at the time, such as the {{ill|Teiō Keizu|ja|帝王系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiō Keizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and incorporated the {{ill|Heavenly Deities|ja|天神七代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3275893}} and {{ill|Earthly Deities|ja|地神五代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Deities|1=Five Generations of Earth Deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18337308}} to complete the work in {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|qid=Q1193078|lt=Ōei|1=Ōei}} 33 (1426). Originally, the genealogy likely extended only to {{ill|Emperor Shōkō|en|Emperor Shōkō|qid=Q451827|lt=Emperor Shōkō|1=Emperor Shōkō}} and was intended to complement {{ill|Tōin Kōtei|ja|洞院公定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōin Kōtei|1=Tōin Kinsada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11555867}}’s earlier work, {{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|en|Sonpi Bunmyaku|qid=Q1704738|lt=Sonpi Bunmyaku|1=Sonpi Bunmyaku}}. The title was inspired by the {{ill|Historical Records of the Southern Song Dynasty|ja|南宋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Records of the Southern Song Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=南宋|qid=Q1147043}}’s "Record of Dynastic Fortunes."
The genealogy begins with the divine age, listing {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} and her successors, followed by {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} and subsequent emperors. It places emphasis on posthumous names, titles, birth mothers, personal names, reign lengths, enthronement details, and burial sites. The format uses horizontal genealogical charts read from right to left, connecting individuals with relationship lines and including brief annotations on their life events and honors.
== History of Revisions == Over the centuries, additions were made during manuscript copies and printings, updating the record to include the reigning emperor at the time. For instance, {{ill|Sanetaka Sanjōnishi|ja|三条西実隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanetaka Sanjōnishi|1=Sanjōnishi Sanetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11355856}} made additions in {{ill|Bunki|en|Bunki|qid=Q1324885|lt=Bunki|1=Bunki}} 2 (1502). Despite textual variations among manuscripts and printed editions, the genealogy remains a key reference for the imperial family’s history.
== Content and Political Context == Unlike the modern {{ill|Kōtōfu|ja|皇統譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtōfu|1=Record of Imperial Lineage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581096}}, the Honchō Kōin Shōunroku includes {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} and the {{ill|Northern Court (Japan)|ja|北朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northern Court (Japan)|1=Northern Dynasties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1207446}} emperors such as {{ill|Emperor Kōgon|ja|光厳院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōgon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 光厳天皇|1=Emperor Kōgon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q471885}} and {{ill|Emperor Kōmyō|ja|光明院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōmyō|1=Emperor Kōmyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11388447}}, while excluding the {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Court (Japan)|1=Southern Dynasties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1206988}} emperors like {{ill|Emperor Go-Murakami|ja|後村上院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Murakami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 後村上天皇|1=Emperor Go-Murakami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q440447}}. This reflects its adherence to the Northern Court's legitimacy, which sought to delegitimize the Southern Court.<ref name="katayama"/>
After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, debates over the legitimacy of the Northern and Southern Courts led to the inclusion of Southern Court emperors in the modern imperial genealogy.
== Structure == The Honchō Kōin Shōunroku adopts a horizontal genealogical structure, listing: * Posthumous names and titles. * Dates of enthronement, abdication, and death. * Birth mothers and significant achievements. * Burial sites and posthumous honors.
== Notable Editions == The most well-known printed edition is part of the {{ill|Gunsho Ruijū|en|Gunsho Ruijū|qid=Q1554852|lt=Gunsho Ruijū|1=Gunsho Ruijū}} series, which extends the genealogy to {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|qid=Q34479|lt=Emperor Shōwa|1=Hirohito}}.
== Legacy == The genealogy served as the basis for the {{ill|Kōtōfu|ja|皇統譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtōfu|1=Record of Imperial Lineage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581096}} until the inclusion of Southern Court emperors in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}. It remains a significant resource for historical research.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōtōfu|ja|皇統譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtōfu|1=Record of Imperial Lineage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581096}} * {{ill|List of emperors of Japan|en|List of emperors of Japan|qid=Q188210|lt=List of emperors of Japan|1=List of emperors of Japan}}
== External Links == * [http://edb.kulib.kyoto-u.ac.jp/exhibit/h679/h679cont.html Kyoto University Library's Honchō Kōin Shōunroku Manuscript (Digital Edition)] * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kotenseki/html/nu02/nu02_00757_0001/index.html Waseda University Library Honchō Kōin Shōunroku (Classic Text Database)]
{{translated page|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|version=97766547|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:本朝皇胤紹運録]]
